food.name: Sticky Bun
food.reason: The namesake item, called 'the star' with detailed praise in a top review; other savory items also praised but sticky buns are the signature standout.
food.quote: warm, soft, perfectly gooey, and just the right balance of sweetness

reviewSample.text:
The Sticky Bun in Deerfield Beach is one of those wonderful little places that instantly feels like a treat. The aroma alone lets you know you’re in for something special, and the baked goods absolutely live up to it.

The sticky buns are the star for a reason—warm, soft, perfectly gooey, and just the right balance of sweetness without being overwhelming. Every bite tastes fresh, homemade, and comforting, like something baked with care rather than rushed out of a kitchen. Portions are generous, making it easy to share… though you may not want to.

What really makes The Sticky Bun shine, though, is that the savory menu is just as satisfying. The smoked turkey with cranberry sauce is outstanding—tender, flavorful turkey paired with a lightly sweet, tangy cranberry sauce that adds just the right contrast. It feels hearty, comforting, and thoughtfully prepared, not like an afterthought.

The Cuban sandwich is another standout. It’s well-pressed, warm, and packed with flavor, with a perfect balance of meats, cheese, and bread. Every bite is satisfying, making it an excellent choice whether you’re stopping in for lunch or a late breakfast.

The atmosphere is casual and welcoming, and the service is friendly and efficient, even when it’s busy. It’s the kind of spot that locals love and visitors are thrilled to discover.
If you’re in Deerfield Beach and enjoy classic baked goods done right, The Sticky Bun is absolutely worth a stop. It’s a simple pleasure that delivers exactly what it promises—and does it beautifully.

Quite busy and cute eclectic breakfast and lunch spot. Can you place your order and then they bring your food to the table. I had the Jersey which brought back memories of my neighbor cooking pork roll when I was young. Have to go back and try some of the other options. I took a look around and everyone's plates look delicious! Young and friendly staff

Very clean establishment. The food was fantastic. Staff super friendly and helpful. Dog friendly also. Live tunes definitely recommend swinging by. You can sit inside or outside. They cut the bacon in house 🤤🤤

drink.name: Coffee
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers praise the coffee, with mentions of great options and non-stop refills.

food.name: Challah French Toast
food.reason: Specifically named in a 5-star review where the reviewer said it hit the spot. Other specific dishes like the meat lover omelet also got strong praise, but French toast is a classic diner highlight here.

reviewSample.text:
This was the most generous, fattest meat lover omelet I've ever had. Ms. Dee went above n beyond to accommodate my annoying demands, from asking for a side of heavycream for my coffee to offering to swap my fruit with sliced avocado, and non-stop hot coffee refills.

My husband ordered the traditional eggs benni. Check out the perfectly medium yolk in the pic.
If you like intimate diner with nostalgic atmosphere, this place is highly recommended.

My husband and I were in Florida for our friend’s wedding and stopped by Sylvain’s Cafe the morning before. I ordered the challah French toast and he ordered the healthy breakfast. Food hit the spot and the service by Kelly and Mel was fantastic. Great coffee options too. Definitely recommend.
Positive experience, good food, great service, cute little hole in the wall diner. If anything could be elevated, the bathroom definitely needs a facelift.

drink.reason: No specific drinks are mentioned in any of the reviews.

food.name: Arancini
food.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review as huge, stuffed with meat, and well-priced at $7. Other reviews rave about authentic Italian food generally, but arancini is the most concretely recommended named item.
food.quote: raving about the Arancini here so I had to try. They were delicious!

reviewSample.text: The best food I’ve had in south Florida! The employees are so nice and the prices are great. You can taste the quality in the food and the meatballs are big and full of flavor. They make everything in house and you can feel the love they carry for their food as soon as you walk in. I highly recommend stopping and eating here or you’ll be missing out big time. Can’t wait to come back

Excited to write this review for authentic Italian food at great prices!
Saw a FB post from local food critic Mikeiseating raving about the Arancini here so I had to try. They were delicious! Some places are skimpy with the meat but these are stuffed. They were HUGE and only $7 each. Also ordered the pear ricotta cake imported from Italy and cannolis which were delicious! Mini cannoli are only $3.50!! The place is very clean, service was excellent by 3 wonderful and friendly employees Rich, Antonio and Andrea. Drove from Hollywood and will be back for sure! Tables outside, open for breakfast and lunch until 4pm with daily specials posted.

Delicious food. Friendly service!
Warm and welcoming! Chef Andrea and Richie were great! As someone who was born and raised in a Sicilian family this food is as authentic as it gets! Patio seating and counter service was very accommodating as well!
Highly recommend this cafe!!!! Can't wait to eat there again!

drink.name: Matcha (vanilla)
drink.reason: Explicitly praised in a 5-star review for its balanced flavor; the only specific drink mentioned positively.
drink.quote: The matcha is the perfect balance of sweet and bitter

food.name: Almond croissant
food.reason: Named and praised across multiple 5-star reviews, including one reviewer who turned around to compliment it.
food.quote: immediately had to turn around to go back in to profess how amazing the croissant was

reviewSample.text:
First time and definitely the last time ordering from Le Petit Poussin.

My boyfriend and I ordered their avocado toast through DoorDash because it looked great in the photos and we were excited to try something new. What arrived was nothing like what was advertised. It was a plain piece of toast with some smashed avocado and an egg on top—no salad, no presentation, and honestly, no flavor. It was extremely bland and underwhelming, and not at all what you’d expect for a $19 menu item.

This wasn’t a case of “presentation being off.” We did not receive the item that was pictured and described on the menu.

We called the store to express our disappointment and were told they couldn’t help us, then given the owner’s number. When we spoke to the owner, he was dismissive and unprofessional, repeatedly stating that presentation is not grounds for a refund and cutting the call short. He showed no interest in actually addressing the issue—that we didn’t receive what we ordered.

Overall, it was a complete letdown in both food quality and customer service. If you’re looking for a good café-style breakfast, Stinky Buns in the Cove and Keke’s are much better options. If you’re looking for a French bakery, Flore Bakery is a great choice.

Save your time and money—this place isn’t worth it.

Amazing food and service!! The matcha is the perfect balance of sweet and bitter. I loved the vanilla with it. And the food is even better. Alonna was awesome and gave me great recommendations like the pistachio roll and almond croissant.

Everything here is perfect (not exaggerating).  The most important in any restaurant is the food and literally everything they make is delicious!  What makes things better is the service, the ladies are so friendly and welcoming (especially Sirine - she’s the best).  There is a gentleman that also helps out and he is also very friendly. They truly seem like they care and are very helpful.

What I typically order…
- almond croissant, chocolate croissant or a combo (sometimes they create new things like the chocolate chip croissant in the pic).  All their croissants are good!
- ham and cheese quiche, it’s just simply the best and they gave you a good size slice with a side salad.  If I need something quick to go then I get the spinach pastry you see in the pic.

TIP: it maybe hard to find from the street because of the name, the name shows “LPP” instead of the full name and it’s at the corner of the plaza.  They also have a cute outdoor area when weather is nice.

Come check it out, you won’t be disappointed!

drink.name: Iced Matcha with Oat Milk
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review; other drink mentions are generic (iced coffee, cold brew, cinnamon honey latte) with only single mentions each.
drink.quote: beverages are excellent- especially the iced matcha with oat milk

food.name: Empanadas
food.reason: Mentioned positively in three separate 5-star reviews, with specific praise calling them a must-try.
food.quote: the empanadas are a must-try—flaky, flavorful

reviewSample.text:
Spectacular little coffee nook destination - always a family favorite to visit while at the beach!
Their beverages are excellent- especially the iced matcha with oat milk!!
And they have a good selection of baked goods.
This is one of my favorite places in Deerfield- always a must visit!
My cousin recommended this little spot for coffee. He was so enthusiastic about it I had to try it. The ice coffee was delicious and had a pork and pineapple empanada that was very tasty.

I found this place while visiting Deerfield Beach. This was perfect small quaint spot located not far from where I was staying. It had a small seating area where it was perfect to sit and enjoy a great cup of coffee. The coffee was tasted fantastic and the staff was so gracious and patient with my order. They made it an easy choice tmto come back everyday I was there. I couldn't have asked for anything more. The highlight was the staff, the fresh coffee and choices and the ability to sit outside and enjoy the beautiful day.

drink.name: Iced Honey Cinnamon Latte
drink.reason: Specifically called out as delicious in a 5-star review; other coffee mentions are generic (Colombian coffee, house brew) though consistently praised.
drink.quote: their iced honey cinnamon latte was delicious

food.name: Avocado Toast with Square Egg
food.reason: Only specifically named food item, praised for quality and presentation in a 5-star review. Other food references (salad, gelato) lack the same specific dish-level praise, though gelato was also enjoyed.
food.quote: avocado toast with a square egg — a fun, modern twist

reviewSample.text: The staff were all so friendly on a busy Saturday afternoon. The coffee was perfect, not too sweet, which is rare. Other coffee spots load up the sugar to cover the flavor. But their iced honey cinnamon latte was delicious. It was clean and well decorated. Excited for their other locations opening up. I will definitely be back!

As an admitted coffee snob, I’m always searching for a place that truly gets coffee right — and Pilar absolutely delivers.
I drink my coffee black, and unlike many chains (hello, over-roasted and bitter), Pilar’s coffee was smooth, balanced, and genuinely enjoyable on its own — no cream or sugar needed.
I stopped in for breakfast and tried their avocado toast with a square egg — a fun, modern twist on a classic — and it was delicious. The quality and presentation really stood out.

What I love most is that Pilar has something for everyone. Whether you're a purist like me who wants a simple, well-crafted cup of coffee, or you're into the trend-forward drinks the younger crowd loves (some with names I can barely pronounce!), they’ve nailed the balance.

I even came back later for dinner, had a great salad, and spent hours working in a beautiful, comfortable environment.
Highly recommend if you're looking for great coffee, great food, and a space you’ll actually want to stay in.

I debated whether to do a review on this place or not cause I don't do these to be negative. But then thought just be real all the time. So the place is dope as far as decor and the service was outstanding. But the food, coffee, and matcha latte were meh, just average nothing that sticks out or would make me come back again. Btw it's in a plaza that has a bunch of decent breakfast spots so the competition is fierce. And it was next to a bar that was open at 8:30 am with patrons which gave me old Florida vibes that I love. Felt nostalgic
